rulings.Case Title: BRAVO & MARGULIES VS. FRANK M. VILCHES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Appl To Stay Action Based On Ca Military And Veterans Code
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANT JEANETTE VILCHES' motion To Stay Action Based On California Military And Veterans Code. Granted for good cause, based on Mil & Vet. Code § 403. On March 7, 2013, Plaintiff filed its non-opposition to the motion. Except for Plaintiff's Motion to Tax Costs that is set for hearing on March 25, 2013 at 9:30 am, this action is stayed for 390 days on account of Ms. Vilches' active deployment. Ms. Vilches' counsel has requested that the opposition to the Motion To Tax Costs go forward without Ms. Vilches present because her appearance is not necessary. The trial date set for March 25, 2013 is vacated, and the parties may set the trial date in Department 206 for any date after the 390 day stay period. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN H. DANNER III et al VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To 4th Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 27, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To 4th Amended Complaint. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Overruled as to 4th through 8th causes of action. The face of the Fourth Amended Complaint does not establish that these causes of action are time barred, and Plaintiffs met the standard for pleading delayed discovery. Further, liberally construing the pleadings, the Court cannot determine on demurrer that Gov. Code 820.2 bars the claims. Overruled as to the 6th cause of action, which sufficiently pleads actual fraud and is not barred by Gov. Code 822.2. Sustained with leave to amend as to the 7th cause of action for Plaintiff to plead a statutory basis for the injury. Sustained without leave to amend on the 9th through 12th causes of action; Plaintiff concedes these claims are barred by Worker's Compensation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIS BOKAN BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, et al VS. THE BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion For Order Disqualifying Pltfs Designated Expert Witness And Pltfs Counsel For Conflict Of Interest
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT THE BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA LIFE & HEALTH's Motion For Order Disqualifying Pltfs Designated Expert Witness And Pltfs Counsel For Conflict Of Interest Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: MATT T. YOURST VS. DAVID L. KANTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion - Yourst To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Admissions; Request For Production Of Documents Qnd To Compel The Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT DAVID KANTER's Motion - Yourst To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories; Request For Admissions; Request For Production Of Documents Qnd To Compel The Production Of Documents And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant DAVID L. KANTER's Motion to Compel and for Sanctions is GRANTED in part. Defendant YOURST shall serve verified responses to the following discovery on or before March 29, 2013: (1) Special Interrogatories, Set One, Interrogatories 11, 14, 15. Defendant YOURST is ordered to appear for deposition on or before March 29, 2013. Defendant KANTER's request for sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 16, PLAINTIFF EGC FINANCIAL, LLC's motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Denied. Plaintiff failed to provide evidence of the substance of the matters "deemed admitted." In any event, the motion appears to be a motion for summary judgment, not a motion for judgment on the pleadings, and it is not in compliance with CCP 437c. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTs TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY & CLIFFORD GILMORE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted in part and denied in part. The motion to strike all references to malice, oppression, and fraud is denied. These claims are not subject to a heightened pleading standard. The motion to strike the claim for punitive damages is denied. The claim is sufficiently pled. The motion to strike all prayers for attorney's fees is granted without leave to amend. The Plaintiff is pro per and not a licensed attorney. The motion to strike the portions of the prayers that are not a demand for judgment is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 425.10 does not require otherwise. The motion to strike paragraph (c) is granted as to the statement "damages for unpaid wages, including regular and overtime wages" with leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating unpaid wages. The motion to strike paragraph (f) of the prayer is granted with leave to amend. The complaint has alleged no facts indicating that plaintiff was denied access to any records. The motion to strike paragraph (g) of the prayer is granted with leave to amend in light of the Court's ruling on the related cause of action for declaratory relief. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANTONIO AMADO VAZ VS. TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTs TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, INC., RANDY SHAW, PRATIBAH TEKKEY & CLIFFORD GILMORE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrer to the first and second causes of action for failure to exhaust administrative remedies is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts showing that he has obtained a right to sue letter from the Department of Fair Housing and Employment for these claims. The demurrer to the first, second, and third causes of action as to the individual defendants Shaw, Tekkey, and Gilmore is sustained without leave to amend. Only employers are liable for these causes of action. The demurrer to the first cause of action for failure to state a claim is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed to allege sufficient facts for a claim of race or national origin discrimination. Plaintiff is given leave to plead further facts that give rise to his claim that he was treated differently on account of race or national origin, and facts concerning acts of discrimination or retaliation on account of his lack of citizenship. The demurrer to the third cause of action for failure to state a claim is overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to state a claim for wrongful discharge based on disability discrimination or retaliation. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. All of the conduct giving rise to the claim stems from the employment relationship and falls within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Worker's Compensation Appeals Board. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting unfair business practices. The demurrer to the sixth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff is given leave to plead, if he can in good faith, facts constituting a present controversy. If Plaintiff files an amended complaint, it must be filed within 20 days after entry of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CASEY FAMILY TRUST et al VS. CASEY SECURITIES , LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action; Notice Of
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, March 14, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANTs CASEY SECURITIES, LLC, GEORGE GASPARININ & JOHN FITZPATRICK's Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. The petition to compel arbitration is denied. The dissolution claim is not subject to arbitration because it is a separate legal mechanism and does not arise out of or relate to the operating agreement. Plaintiff's citations to Georgia and Montana law are persuasive. The Court denies FINRA arbitration because the dissolution does not arise out of FINRA business activities. The Court rejects defendants' estoppel argument. Plaintiff never agreed to submit the dissolution issue to arbitration. The fraudulent transfer claim is directly related to the dissolution and therefore, it is not subject to arbitration. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
